rule_description: Sum of amide, carbamate, urea, and sulfonamide counts as a polar P3/P4 substituent proxy.
Physicochemical pass count plus the four structural motif flags.
1 if amide-like polar substituents suggest a P3/P4 hydrogen-bonding handle.
Count of satisfied hydrophobic P2 proxy conditions.
Count of conserved structural motif proxies among warhead, P1, P2, and P3/P4.
1 if hydrophobic branched/cyclic P2-sidechain proxies are present.
Count of non-ring sp3 carbons attached to at least two carbons.
1 if all physicochemical windows from the source response are satisfied.
Count of passed physicochemical rules derived from the source response.
Count of non-aromatic carbocyclic rings of size 3-6 as a hydrophobic sidechain proxy.
1 if gamma-lactam or donor/acceptor-rich amide motifs suggest the conserved S1 H-bond pattern.
1 if any electrophilic warhead proxy is present.
Sum of nitrile, aldehyde, ketone, and Michael-acceptor motif counts as a warhead proxy.
Five-membered lactam count as a P1 glutamine-mimic proxy.
1 if a five-membered lactam P1 glutamine-mimic proxy is present.
Formal charge from the input graph.
Spiro atom count as an additional rigid-sidechain proxy.
Bridgehead atom count as a rigid bicyclic-sidechain proxy.
Alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl count as a Michael-acceptor proxy.
